Alrighty, hey I've watched Russell sell products he knows nothing about many times. Think about that. The product rarely sells itself. Okay? Don't fall in love with the product so much that you forget how to sell a thing. That's what this whole event's been about. So while yes, the product has to be amazing and you make it into an offer and it does everything that you promise it will do and more, you've got to make sure that you understand that your product is not good enough to sell itself. Okay? So what this is going to go through, it's going to show you how to actually sell stuff and you're going to see stuff that you don't even know about. Especially tomorrow, he's going to choose someone randomly. We're going to go through and we're actually going to have him sell your product which is crazy and he'll know nothing about it. I watched him sell a product as if he was a pregnant woman last time. It was insane. Many, anyway, lots of things I've got. So anyways, think about the power of that. So what Russell's going to do is he's going to come on in and he's going to show you a stack. Watch what he does both with his inflections, right? Remember, watch that video. His energy level, he's going to see the pressure and the speed that he does start to increase. All the things, remember we talked about the buy now, the NLP things he does. He's going to walk you through that every, through all the pieces. Definitely record this. And anyway, I'm very excited. You guys excited for this? This is 14 years of mastered experience that's going to come at you. You're going to witness this for about two hours. Okay? This is awesome stuff. The stack itself usually takes him about 30 to 40 minutes. He's going to dive deep with us for a couple of hours on this. Guys, please stand up and welcome to stage Mr. Russell Brunson. So I'm just going to recap what we've done in the last two days because it's been a lot but it's also not that much. Okay? So I know you guys are going to slide by slide home and there's a lot of stuff and figuring things out and it takes a while, right? But we break it down to the essence of what we've done so far is you guys have figured out your origin story, your story about your vehicle, internal and your external, right? So again, the whole structure of the perfect webinar is that it's telling four stories. If I've done it right, the dominoes knocked over, they believe that this is the right vehicle for them. They believe that they can do it and they believe that all external faults please throw away. Now they're like, and you'll see people in the chat box saying like, just take my money. Like it literally will happen almost every single time because they've got belief now. And now all you have to do is make them a really, really good offer. Don't screw it up. Okay? If you're not careful, you'll screw it up. People get so annoyed that they'll just not buy from you. And I learned that the hard way by not making a lot of money for a long time. And then luckily I met people who understood this process, who were good at it, who coached me through it. And then for the last decade, since then it's been always learning and adding and learning and adding and learning and adding to where it's at today. And that's what we're going to be sharing with you guys. One like most important story I want to tell before we dive into this whole thing is the concept, before we talk about it before, it's called the stack. And the reason why we call it the stack is I learned it from a guy named Armand Moran and he called it the stack. So that's it. And it was interesting because I had been speaking at events for probably a year and a half or so at that time and I got on stage and I was good. I wasn't great but I was doing good. I was able to convince people to buy and it was a good living, right? And Armand Moran had at the time the biggest event in our industry. And I always wanted to speak out of my whole life. It was the first thing I ever went to and I was like, ah, somebody I want to speak at and like four or five years into it, he asked me if I'd speak and I was so excited. And so I was preparing for getting ready and two weeks before his event, he was speaking at a Dan Kennedy event. So once Dan Kennedy's event and they had like 1500 people in the room or something and Armand spoke on day four after like 200 speakers had all like pitched stuff to this audience and I watched person after person and like half of them didn't do, didn't sell very well and just kind of ups and downs of the whole thing and a couple did okay but for most part it was kind of bad. And Armand got there the last day and I was like, these guys are all beat up. Like it was one of those kind of, who's been to those kind of events? Yeah, I met you at one of those events. Yes, and I came and saved it for day four of that event and I came in and closed everybody and then that's when we met you guys. Anyway, so Armand came in to sing and I was just like, good luck man, these guys have been beat up and he's like, I'm not worried. And he gets there, he does a little presentation and he sells and at the end I see the entire audience like probably 40 to 50% of people run to the back of the room, they're buying stuff and I'm just watching like in amazement. I have no idea what he did or how he did, I was just like that was the coolest thing I ever saw. And then I kind of hung out waiting for someone to talk to me afterwards for all the people to kind of leave and then he saw me in the corner, so he started walking over to me and first thing he did is he grabbed his stack of order forms. And when you speak on stage they give you a big stack of order forms and every order form is equivalent of like a thousand dollar bill or $2,000, whatever you're spelling, right? He's got this big old fat stack of order forms, he comes over to me and I was like, that's your order forms? He's like, yeah man, I guess how much that is. I was like, I don't know. And it was like 300 and something thousand dollars, something great. I was just like, dude, that is like, I couldn't even fathom it. And he's like, hey, in two weeks, you speak my event, do you want a big stack of order forms or do you want a small stack of order forms? I was like, I want a big stack of order forms. He's like, okay, this is the difference between big stack of order forms and small stack. The small stack, you'll do your clothes the way you do it right now. Big stack, if you want a big stack of order forms, you do the stack the way I do it. I'm like, okay, what do you do? He's like, didn't you see me just do it? I'm like, I think so? I don't know what you did, but it worked really, really good. And he explained this concept to me. He said that almost every speaker at the time in the speaking industry was out there. What they would do is they go through whatever, and everyone's got different versions of this. This is kind of my blend of what I figured out. So everyone's got something different here. They teach whatever happens in the front and they transition to selling. What they typically do is they have some kind of package they put together. So this is the first thing you're going to get. It's so awesome. This is what you want. And then here's the second thing you're going to get. And here's the third thing you're going to get. Here's the fourth thing you're going to get. And here's the fifth thing you're going to get. And then here's my special bonus you're going to get. And it's going to cost you this. I'm not going to charge you that. I'm going to charge you that. And then they sell. And so the problem is that a human mind can only remember the very last thing it was told. You can't remember all these things. So what happens is that you talk about this cool thing and this cool thing and this cool thing and this cool thing and this cool thing and then the last thing you share, which is like bonus 22, which is like the stupidest thing in your entire offer. And then you're like, the last bonus. And then it costs you know $15,000. And people are like, that's not worth $15,000. The only thing they remember is the last thing you offered them. So what he invented was this thing called the stack. So the way the stack works is basically I talk about the first thing in my offer. I get them all excited. Tell a story, short testimonials. And then I have a slide called a stack slide. So basically this means you're going to get this. And I have the first thing in there. Then I transition to the second thing. I talk about it, go through the whole thing. And then when I'm done I go back and say, so that means you're going to get this and this. And I stack it. And here's the third thing you're going to get. And that means you're going to get this, this, and this. The fourth thing. So the very end, before I transitioned the price, I said, I go to the very last thing and that means you're going to get boom, boom, boom, boom. You see this huge entire thing and you're like, holy cow, that's the whole thing. And that's what's making it to the price. And all of a sudden the entire offer is actually worth the thing. And that was the stack. He said, do that. So I went home and I didn't really remember how he did it. I was trying to figure it out. So I did it. Went to his vent the next two weeks later. Spoke on stage at $160,000 in sales in 90 minutes. And the way it works, you get half and the speaker gets half or the event promoter gets half. So I walked home with $80,000 in order forms. And I had never made $80,000 an hour my whole life, let alone my entire year. And I was just like, oh my gosh, the stack is the greatest thing in the world. I will never not do this. And I did it for a while. So I started going back and I started adding it to my tele seminars back then and webinars and started using it for a long time. But something weird happened. And this is going to happen to you guys. So I'm warning you right now. Because if you listen to this voice, then your head that'll come, I promise you it'll come, you will lose all the money. If you ignore this voice, you'll continue to make money. I kept doing it. And then one day I was like, I feel so cheesy doing this. It's so goofy. Like, I don't like it. I'm just going to not do it anymore. I'm too sophisticated for this or whatever I thought. I don't know. So I stopped doing the stack. And I tried it once in an event and I bombed. Nobody moved. I tried it again. And I tried it again after three or four times. I was like, OK, I am now making a rule for myself. I will never still think ever again in the history of my life without doing the stack and I haven't since. It works. And the first time you feel goofy, it'll be kind of weird. But the difference between $10,000 and $100,000 or $100,000 and a million, it's worth it every single time. Just do it. Get good at it. Practice it. Practice it in the mirror. Whatever it takes, it's the key. It's what, when this domino comes down, it causes so much desire that they just have to give you money. So that's the foundation where we talk about. So I'm going to do the stack. I'm going to do the funnel hack stack. But if you look at any presentation I've ever done in the last decade, they all follow the same process. These tweak here and there, but it's almost the same. In fact, when I built the software secrets slides and the other ones, like I use the same slides and I delete things, move things around, so the framework doesn't change. The stories change. The testimonials change, things like that. But the most part, it's pretty solid. That make sense? Any questions about it at all? OK, feel free during this time to ask questions if you guys have them. This quick question. When you're showing me your first thing, like for master class, are you showing the value of saying that's worth this? Or do you wait until you've stacked it and then show the value? Because I find when I show the value of something like, yeah, this is a six-week master class and the value is 2,997. Sometimes I see a little bit of a drop off the webinar when I do that. Then I only get to see my whole offer. I just wonder if there's a way to show the value of each one once it's stacked and then put the whole price. I do it immediately. The people who leave the webinar, those are the people who aren't going to buy. The people who stick the ones that this got knocked down and they're excited, the ones that are like, eh. So I wouldn't worry too much about it. The way I do my numbers too, and this is the metrics to measure your webinar by. During this whole thing, if you look at, here's a timeline. My webinar typically, this piece right here takes about one hour. I spend 15 minutes on my origin story, 15 minutes on secret one, two, three, and that's an hour. I make this transition now to my close. My close takes about 30 minutes and then my Q&A takes about 30 minutes. So that's kind of the timeline. So it's about a two hour presentation typically. When I transition from all this stuff to right here, every time I transition, I look at how many people are still on when I'm transitioning to the close. I look at a number that says there's 1,000 people. For me, I know exactly, I always close 15% of what that is. So I know that 1,000 people, 150 people are going to buy whatever I'm selling. With this presentation, not all my presentations, but the funnel hacks presentation is 15%. I've had the ones that are 10, other ones are five. I always tell people, if you got about 5% of these people who are on the call to action, you've got like a six figure year of business. If you get 10%, that's like a million dollar year of business, you get 15%, that's 10 plus million bucks. That's the difference. And so, and all, like the, getting better from here to here to here is just the stack part. Like this is it. So it's worth, like 5% increase is a lot more money, like 10x money, 10x money. So every percent increase you get is a lot. So all these little things I'm going to be telling you, I'm going to drop in a whole bunch of stuff. Some will apply to you, some won't apply. Let's just say you own, like, you own, I have to take a second to get in the state on this. Sorry. Okay, so let's say you totally, totally screwed up and you only get 10% of the 10%. How many of you guys were next to that? That's like $1,000 a day. How many of you guys are next to $1,000 a day to change the quality of life pretty well right now? That's $30,000 a month. What would you use next to $30,000 a month? Okay, but some of you guys are like, man, that's a lot, Russ. Like, I'm living paycheck to paycheck right now, right? I just started this business, or you have all these fears, right? I totally get that. So let's say you totally, totally, totally screwed up and you only get 10% of the 10% of the 10% of the Venus factory guys. You only make an extra $100 a day. It's $3,000 a month. What would you do next to $3,000 a month? Do I cover a car payment, a house payment? You get a new car, right? What would you do next to $3,000 a day? Or $3,000 a month? Like, most people, that would dramatically change the quality of life. Okay? So the point of this is you don't have to be, you don't have to get it perfect to be successful. You can totally screw this up as long as you follow the process, you can still be successful, okay? All right. So that's the first slide, okay? So I'm transitioning. If you look at how people's brains work, right? In NLP, it talks about there's two types of people, right? There's people that move towards pleasure and there's people who move away from pain. How many of you guys make decisions in your life moving towards pleasure? How many of you guys make decisions in your life moving away from pain? Okay? Typically, most people are hardwired for one or the other, okay? The reason why most entrepreneurs screw up their presentation, most entrepreneurs make their decisions because they want to move towards pleasure. Most of society makes their decisions because they want to move away from pain, okay? So we come in there like, you're going to make a million dollars a day and you're going to be rich, you're going to, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, right? Or you come in there like, you're going to have a sexy body and ripped abs and da, da, da, da, da, right? Very small percentage of the audience is like, sweet, I want to live on a boat and I want to have six pack abs and all those things, right? But because we're entrepreneurs and that's what drives us, that's what we always pitch hard, okay? So you have to understand that we are not the majority of most people. Some people are there. So if you look at this the way I did it is I painted a picture for people who are moving towards pleasure like, what an extra, you guys make $150,000 a day. What would that be like making us $150,000 a day? And my 10% of my moving towards pleasure, people are like, oh my gosh, I'm getting a plane, a jet, a boat, a yacht and it's going to be the most amazing in the world, right? And everyone else is like, I just hate my boss. So then I go to the next level, the next level, the next level, the bottom end of this, I'm just like, man, an extra like $100 a day, that's $3,000 a month, what would you do with that? Would they pay off your credit card bills? That get you out of pain? Could you fire your boss? Could you actually make this your job? Work at home with your kids? And my away from pain people are like, oh my gosh, like if I could fire my boss, I hate that guy. That'd be awesome, right? So I'm talking to both ends of the spectrum here, okay? Be very, very careful if you're only talking towards pleasure or away from pain. The blend of the two is the key because just everyone acts differently. And most entrepreneurs lean towards the pleasure and that's why they alienate a lot of their audiences in every market. So that's the one. Any questions about that slide at all? This is me kind of recapping what my whole premise of this thing is, if you funnel hack somebody, that's the secret. You find someone successful, you model them, you find who their ads are, you get similar ads and that's the whole thing I taught in the last hour in a nutshell. He can do that? It'd be the same thing in software secrets when I showed the whole thing. So all you got to do then is you're going to doodle out your dream software and then you're going to take that doodle and you're going to give it to the wireframe section, wireframe, then you give it to UI guy, he's an UI guy in the Hydro Programmer and the genius will build your thing. Can you do that? Because all you got to do is doodle. That's the only step you got to do. If you could do that, you could do that. And then let's say you're the same kind of thing. What would it be like if you did this or this or this? I'm trying to paint that picture right there. Any questions about that? All right, so that's the first thing. Now, number two, oh, then I come back. So again, over the last hour, we've talked about a lot of things, but we recapped really quickly talked about it. The first thing we talked about was funnel hacking. I showed you guys how to ethically steal over a million dollars from funnel hacks from your competitors for under $100. Number two, we talked about funnel cloning. I showed you how you can then clone the approval funnel and set a click funnel in less than 10 minutes. Number three, I'm going to show you guys my number one traffic hack, how to do the exact same customers, or currently go to your competitors' funnels, start coming to your funnel instead. So I recapped the same thing with the stack. I want them to re-remember everything they've learned at this point and be like, oh yeah, okay, boom. And they have that re-brings in the whole context of last hour and re-reminds them like, okay, that kind of brings all this stuff back to their remembrance. Now they've got it. Now I'm going to start transitioning over to the close. Okay. And this is where most people get super nervous and super awkward. I see stage presenters all the time. They get down to thinking they're teaching and all of a sudden they're like, I'm going to sell something. And they get really, really nervous. They start freaking out. They start hyperventilating. They start, yeah. And then they apologize and they feel all nervous and it's like the most awkward train wreck in the world. I feel like my favorite time ever, I was at some people that go to church with us, call us and we're like, hey, we're doing a doTERRA party at our house. You want to come and we'll try on some essential oils. And I was like, my wife's like, I don't know if I'm going to go. I hate those kind of parties. I was like, please let me go with you. Please, I want to go so bad. And so I was the only dude in the room full of women and they're all trying on oils. And the lady presenting was amazing. Like, talking about the oils and how they work and how it's changed her life and cured cancer and all the things they do, right? And I was just like, she was really good presenting. And then it got to the point where she was going to start selling something. And all of a sudden she starts talking faster and getting nervous. Her face turns all red. And if you guys want to buy a little bit of it, it says like, I don't know, probably a 20 minute pitch in like two and a half seconds. And I'm so sorry. But if you want the forms back there, don't worry about it, you don't have to buy it. But if you want them there, I got to go. And it just ended. I felt so bad for her because that's the hardest thing. We don't know how to transition. And I struggled with it for a long time. I feel the pain of like, how do you make that awkward transition? How many of you guys have done a presentation and you've gotten stuck in that awkward spot? So this is how I do it. It's super easy. You get done teaching, you recap it, and you stop. And all you say is, let me ask you a question real quick. That's the transition. You guys all say it? Let me ask you a question real quick. OK, that's it. And they're like, yeah, what? And then I try to have something there to kind of break the thing. So for me, I usually show this picture right here. So I say, let me ask you a question real quick. We're going for an hour. How many of you guys feel a little bit like this right now? And everyone laughs. I know I talk really, really fast and I get excited. I know I've crammed so much stuff in the last hour. But I wanted to give as much information as possible to you during the time we had together. One time I was speaking at an event and I got off stage and this little man ran up to me and said, Russell, this is so exciting. I feel like I had a fire hose in my mouth the entire time. And next week, some guy on my team sent me this picture. I was like, that's what people feel like after they talk to me, right? How many of you guys feel a little bit like that? OK, I totally get it. So because of that, I don't want you to leave overwhelmed. I just want to inspire you and show you what's possible. So what I actually did is I, and then this comes back to if I'm on an event, I talked to Grant Cardone and we figured out a package we could put together so that you guys can take this stuff and when you get back home, you can actually implement it. Are you guys a cool 5-spend, about 10 or 15 minutes, walking through the special offer made for you guys? Absolutely. Good. And I stop. Don't get nervous. I ask for their permission. This is the key. This is the secret. If you don't ask for their permission, it's awkward. People will start popping up and walking out and they will leave in the middle of your presentation. It's the worst thing in the world and you get more nervous. You see people leaving. It's just super weird and super awkward. So I acknowledged. I said, look, I know that you couldn't figure it. I showed you what's possible, but you need to figure out how to actually do this. I can't do that in a 90-minute presentation I have here. We've got to do this offline. I want to go with you to do that. So because of that, me and then whoever the promoter was, it was my own, we put together stuff. If it's my own, I say, hey, we put together a really special offer for you. I'm a host with somebody else. I was talking to So-and-So and we had a special offer and he said he was okay with me presenting it as long as you guys were okay with it. Is she okay if I spend a few minutes going over it? And then you wait. And if they don't say anything, sometimes they won't, you say, cool, I already know all this stuff, so if you guys don't want it, I'm cool. I can just go home. I'm good. And then they're all like, no, no, no, they always do it. So almost every time they'll say yes when you ask permission and the times that they don't, that's how you do it. Like, okay, sure, because I already know stuff. I'm good. I can go right now. And they always say something because they feel bad. But as soon as they say that, as soon as they say yes and give you permission to sell, nobody moves out of their seats. It's the craziest thing in the world. They will all sit there and then they'll listen to you as you make your special offer. Okay? But if you don't ask them permission, it's this awkward, weird thing. Okay? So, I'll do it one more time real quick. So, how many of you guys feel a little bit like this? Cool. Because of that, we put together a really special offer to help you take all the stuff you figured out in the last 90 minutes and be able to go home and be able to implement this so you can have the same results in your business, in your life, in your health, whatever your business is. I mean, you guys look at if I spent about 10 or 15 minutes going through that real quick, you guys? Yeah. All right, cool. Thank you. I always like to ask permission. I never want to assume I've earned it, but I definitely know this is something that's going to be awesome for you. Boom, then I move over. Okay? All right, so what we created for you is this really cool thing called Funnel Hacks. Okay, now I start transitioning it. Any questions so far about that? That will like make this part now fun because everyone's giving you permission like, please sell me. I'm going to do a webinar. People are like, take my money. Where can I send you money? It's the coolest thing in the world. You see that and you're like, okay, all right, we're doing this good. They want to hear it and they're going to sit and they're going to be present and there's not this weirdness of like, the worst thing I have presenters, they're speaking and they start transitioning to a closed middle. I'm like, are they selling you something? Like, ah, I've never felt that before and you feel dirty. Like this guy, he's seriously selling me. I feel like I got bait and switched. Because otherwise, it's like, no, I gave you my heart and my soul. You know exactly what to do. But if you want me to show you how to do it, like I got something really cool. You guys are cool if I take a moment and share that with you, right? So that's the thing. So you ask permission, the cell does not get awkward, nobody leaves and your closing rate increases dramatically. Okay, yes? Do you announce how long it will take? I usually say about 10 to 15 minutes. You say that. Yeah. Which it could do if I spoke really, really fast. But usually, yeah, it usually takes about 30 minutes. If I said 30 minutes, they're probably like, yeah, but if I say 10 to 15 minutes, they're like, oh, cool. So, okay. All right, so the thing we put together is called funnel hacks. Now, what you're going to get when you invest in funnel hacks today, okay, is sumptive language. If you buy my product today, no. When you invest in funnel hacks today, this is where you're going to get. From this point forward, I've assumed they're buying. Everything I say is a sumptive. When you invest in funnel hacks today, this is what you're going to get. When you invest in funnel hacks today, when you invest in my program today, this is what you're going to get. Never say if at this point forward. Everything from here is something they're buying. When you invest in funnel hacks today, this is what you're going to get. The first thing you're going to get is access to the entire Edison suite for free. How many of you want click funnels for free? Now, not only do you click funnels for free the next six months, I'm also going to give you click funnels, backpack, and action edicts. One thing, when you're creating offers, my goal and every offer is to always make everything I'm selling free, even if it costs a lot of money. Okay? Myron gets it. Okay. If you look at the way we structure this presentation, we used to sell click funnels. The presentation was like converting around 5% to 10%. When I gave away click funnels for free, when they invested in my funnel hacks training, it jumped up to 15%. From a six-figure a year business to a $10 million business. It's all about how you structure the offer. They are investing in this, the training program. When they buy the training program, I'm giving them access to click funnels, backpack, and action edicts for free for the next six months. That makes sense? I want whatever I'm selling to be for free. Everything else, besides, they're paying $1,000 for free. Everything else is free. I want to be able to save for you a billion times during this close. When they invest in this, they all are for free. When you invest in funnel hacks today, the first thing you're going to get access to the entire Edison Suite, click funnels, backpack, and action edicts for free for the next six months. How many of us want to get click funnels for free for the next six months? Everyone's going crazy. I didn't even have time to talk about backpack or action edicts. These programs are just as cool as not cool when you click funnels. I'm going to throw those in for free as well. Now, inside of our click funnels account, our enterprise members, what they get inside of their account, they can create just 70 funnels. They can have 20,000 contacts, 100,000 monthly visitors, and a whole bunch more. What I'm doing right here is I'm trying to create a constraint that later my bonus can let go of that constraint. When I get to the end of my stack, remember this slide, because the end of the stack, what my bonus is, is for any of you guys who get started in the next 48 hours, the first 50 or whatever my thing is, I'm going to take this constraint off. This is pretty cool. I can have 70 funnels, 20,000 contacts, 100,000 monthly visitors, and more. Their mind with the thing is, what if I create 100 funnels? Amazing cost. They all freak out. None of them ever do, but they always are like, I don't want any limits. I try to introduce constraint here so I can take that constraint off later. Does that make sense? That's the key. I'm going through here, this is where you're going to get this, this, this, and they're all excited. In the back of the head, they're like, what happens if I make funnel number 71 or if I have 30,000 contacts, what's going to happen? It's when I release that constraint at the end, that's when they go nuts. That's when the masses start. That's when people are sprinting over the table, jumping over themselves. I had people throw credit cards at me. The best sales pitch we ever did, I was at a supplement thing, and they were having a wedding afterwards or something. I can't remember. I was supposed to have 90 minutes and I get there, they're like, oh, you only have an hour. An hour? It takes me nine, I got to go through this and everything else. Sorry, man, there's literally a wedding coming in the second you're done. We had to clear the tables, get everybody out. Dude, that's not fair. I actually was going to not do the event. I was going to go teach and I'm not going to sell anything. Dave Woodward, luckily, was with me. Dave's like, no, man, we had to fly somewhere and drive two hours. He's like, we came all the way here, we're selling something. Then I'm like, okay, fine, I'm going to get the order forms. The promoter didn't even have order forms. I'm like, what are we supposed to do? Dave's like, I'll take care of it, man. He opened up his computer in Microsoft Word and he typed out first name and then like, credit card number, shipping address, whatever. Then it said, funnel hacks order form. That was literally it. Then he went down to the business center and printed them all out and handed me a mic. We can't get people these forms. This is so not real. He's like, dude, we're selling. I don't care what you say. I'm like, ugh. I went through this whole thing and I got to this stack. I had maybe three minutes through the entire stack and I see the wedding party outside. They're coming in and the promoter's like, you need to get off stage. You're going to find me. I'm like, I'm going as fast as I can. In the middle of it, like, as soon as I did the release of the constraints, some guy from the back, he went and grabbed an order form, filled it out and runs to the front and smacks it on the stage. I was like, this guy knows what he's doing. Also, everyone else was like, and they start running back, running back, grabbing order forms, filling them out, running back and slamming it in. I'm doing the stack and the close and people are sprinting to the front, slamming down order forms on the table. I'm just like, this is the greatest thing in the world. We had no film crew to film it, but they're coming in and Dave's like, just dying in the back. He's throwing forms like, come on guys, come on, let's go. I just kept going and fine. I'm like, okay, the people are coming in. I'm like, okay, well, we got to go. I didn't get to finish the last four or five slides. I was like, I got to go, you guys. Everyone else is in. If you guys aren't in, you're crazy, they have to go and they never kept coming in and slamming them up. We ended up closing in. It was like 60-something percent of the room from that insanely thing that happened. To cause the table rush and those kind of things, it's like the introduction of constraints and then letting them go. It gets people to jump over tables and fight to get to your thing, which is outside of winning a wrestling match, which is the greatest high I've ever had in my entire life, the second greatest thing is watching a table rush. There's something about that where it's like, I can't believe everyone. This is awesome. This is what I do. I'm introducing the constraint here so I can release the constraint later. That make sense? Okay. Every business has, it's going to be different every business, but if you can figure that piece out, that's magic. Kalen's webinar that kills it, you get a year access to the weight loss app. That's the constraint. At the end of it, it's like, hey, if you act now, you get lifetime access. Oh, women go crazy. It's an app. There's only 30 meal plans in it. They rotate every 30 days. You always have lifetime access to it anyway. Anyway. Okay.
